stories.headline: April hacks hit $620M in 20 days — worst month since Bybit's Feb '25 breach
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols have lost $620M to hacks and exploits in the first 20 days of April, eclipsing Q1 2026 combined.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: 95% of losses came from just two incidents — bridge and admin-key failures, not novel zero-days — meaning the playbook is repeatable and the next nine-figure drain is likely already in motion.
the_details: Year-to-date theft sits at ~$771.8M across 47 separate incidents, per Live Bitcoin News tallies.
Drift on Solana ($285M, Apr 1) and Kelp DAO ($292M, Apr 18) account for nearly all of April's losses.
April's total is 3.7x larger than Q1 2026 combined, the worst since the $1.4B Bybit breach in Feb 2025.

stories.headline: BlackRock IBIT options open interest tops Deribit — derivatives center of gravity flips
stories.tldr: Open interest on BlackRock's IBIT bitcoin ETF options surpassed Deribit on Friday, marking a structural shift to U.S. regulated venues.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: When the deepest BTC options book lives inside a U.S. brokerage wrapper, hedging flows and implied vol get set by TradFi desks — not crypto natives — permanently changing how spot reacts to macro events.
the_details: IBIT options launched in November 2024 and have now overtaken Deribit, the long-time offshore leader.
Milestone hit on Apr 24, 2026, as BTC traded near $80K with whales building long perpetual positions.
Signals rapid institutional adoption of regulated crypto derivatives less than 18 months post-launch.

stories.headline: 100+ crypto firms led by Coinbase, Ripple press Senate to mark up Market Structure bill
stories.tldr: A coalition of 100+ crypto firms is pushing the Senate Banking Committee to advance the CLARITY Act and finalize a federal framework for digital assets.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Industry alignment on a single bill is rare — if the Senate moves before midterm season, U.S. crypto firms get the SEC/CFTC jurisdiction split they've waited 7 years for, unlocking listings and custody decisions held in limbo.
the_details: Letter signed by Coinbase, Ripple and 100+ others, delivered week of April 23, 2026.
Builds on the SEC-CFTC joint interpretive release issued March 17, 2026 — a 68-page rewrite superseding the 2019 framework.
CLARITY Act roundtable already held April 16; bill defines token taxonomy across five categories.

stories.headline: Kelp DAO loses $292M from rsETH bridge — Aave hit by liquidity crunch
stories.tldr: An attacker drained $292M from Kelp DAO's rsETH token on April 19, stranding wrapped ether across 20 chains and triggering heavy Aave withdrawals.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The cascade from a single LRT bridge into a major lending market exposed how restaking risk is now systemic — DeFi blue chips can't isolate themselves when collateral spans 20 bridges.
the_details: Largest DeFi exploit of 2026 — $292M drained from Kelp DAO's rsETH on Apr 18-19.
Wrapped ether stranded across 20 chains, sparking 'DeFi is dead' narrative across crypto Twitter.
Aave saw heavy withdrawals as users pre-emptively de-risked LRT-backed positions.

stories.headline: SEC carves out DeFi front-ends from broker-dealer registration
stories.tldr: SEC staff said on April 13 it won't object to 'Covered User Interface Providers' offering software to submit crypto-asset securities transactions without registering as broker-dealers.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: This is the most explicit greenlight U.S. DeFi has ever received — projects can run hosted front-ends on U.S. infrastructure without needing FINRA membership, removing the cleanest legal threat to onchain trading UIs.
the_details: Statement issued April 13, 2026 by SEC staff covering 'Covered User Interface Providers'.
Exempts UIs that let users prepare and submit crypto asset security transactions from broker-dealer registration.
Pairs with the SEC-CFTC joint framework released March 17 — both moves aimed at codifying onchain market structure.
